epics.title: Sales Tax Accrual by Jurisdiction
epics.description: Automate sales tax tracking across multiple jurisdictions (state, county, city) with GL integration

epics.title: 1099 Contractor Report Generator
epics.description: Generate IRS Form 1099-MISC and 1099-NEC reports for contractor payments with FIRE export

epics.title: Commission Tracking & Reporting
epics.description: Track and report employee commissions across sales, F&I, and service departments

epics.title: Floor Plan Financing Module
epics.description: Track floored inventory with daily interest accrual and automated payoff

user_stories.title: Tax jurisdiction configuration with GL account mapping
user_stories.story: Given a controller configures tax jurisdiction rates in the system, When they set state=CA, county=ALAMEDA, city=OAKLAND with 8.625% rate and effective date, Then the system stores the rate with the GL payable and receivable account mappings
acceptance_criteria: Tax jurisdiction code is unique per tenant and effective date
GL account IDs are validated (must exist and be active)
Effective date can be in the past or future
Historical rates are preserved for audit trail
Rate changes do NOT retroactively recalculate historical entries

user_stories.title: Automatic tax accrual on deal posting
user_stories.story: Given a deal with taxable service ($1,000) and parts ($2,000) is posted, When the deal posts to GL, Then the system automatically creates a journal entry debiting tax receivable and crediting tax payable based on jurisdiction tax rate
acceptance_criteria: Tax accrual entry is created atomically with deal posting
Entry flows through GL Integrity Agent for validation
Tax amount is calculated per jurisdiction effective rate
Entry is reversible if deal is chargebacked
Audit trail records journal entry ID and accrual date

user_stories.title: Tax-exempt customer management
user_stories.story: Given a customer has a valid resale certificate, When the certificate is uploaded and stored in the system with expiration date, Then all subsequent deals are flagged as tax-exempt and no tax accrual entry is created
acceptance_criteria: Certificate can be uploaded as PDF/image
Certificate document URL is stored with customer record
System checks certificate expiration on each deal posting
Expired certificates require re-upload before next tax-exempt deal
Audit trail records who uploaded certificate and when

user_stories.title: Monthly tax liability report for EOM
user_stories.story: Given a controller initiates month-end close, When ACCT_062 (Sales Tax Report Generation) executes, Then the system generates a Sales Tax Liability Report grouped by jurisdiction with amounts due
acceptance_criteria: Report shows month accruals vs. total due (month + prior unpaid)
Report groups by state, county, city, district
Report matches state filing portal format requirements (CA, TX, FL, NY)
GL trial balance snapshot is appended for audit
Report is exportable to CSV and email-deliverable

user_stories.title: Multi-tenant tax isolation
user_stories.story: Given a dealer group with 5 rooftops (5 separate tenants), When tax accrual runs, Then each tenant's tax is calculated independently and isolated by tenant_id
acceptance_criteria: All tax_jurisdictions queries include WHERE tenant_id = X
Tax accrual calculations use tenant-specific GL accounts
Consolidated group report shows tax by rooftop
No cross-tenant data leakage in any query

user_stories.title: Tax rate changes with effective dates
user_stories.story: Given tax rates change on a future date (e.g., CA raises sales tax on 2026-07-01), When the controller updates the rate in the system with effective_date=2026-07-01, Then deals posted before that date use the old rate and deals posted on/after use the new rate
acceptance_criteria: System maintains rate history with effective dates
Rate lookup queries use deal posting date to find applicable rate
No retroactive recalculation of prior deals
Audit log shows when rate was changed and by whom
Multiple rates can exist for same jurisdiction at different effective dates

user_stories.title: Vendor 1099 eligibility flag
user_stories.story: Given a vendor record with a tax ID (TIN) and '1099-eligible' flag checked, When the controller reviews vendor master, Then the system marks them as eligible for 1099 generation
acceptance_criteria: Vendor master includes 1099-eligible boolean flag
TIN field is required for 1099-eligible vendors
Flag can be toggled on/off without deleting vendor
Audit trail records when flag was changed

user_stories.title: Automatic 1099 generation for tax year
user_stories.story: Given it is January 2027, When the controller clicks 'Generate 1099s for 2026', Then the system queries all 1099-eligible vendors with TIN, sums AP check payments, and generates 1099-MISC/1099-NEC forms for vendors with $600+
acceptance_criteria: System queries vendor_1099_records and sums AP payments by vendor TIN
Only vendors with $600+ in total payments are included
1099-MISC is generated for rent/royalty vendors
1099-NEC is generated for non-employee compensation vendors
Initial status is DRAFT (not FILED)
Generation is idempotent (running twice doesn't create duplicates)

user_stories.title: Vendor consolidation across rooftops by TIN
user_stories.story: Given a vendor has payments from rooftop A ($800) and rooftop B ($400) in 2026, When 1099 generation runs, Then all payments are summed by vendor TIN across all tenants in the dealer group
acceptance_criteria: System groups vendors by TIN (not by individual vendor_id per tenant)
Payments from all tenants with same TIN are aggregated
Single 1099 is generated for $1,200 total
Rooftop attribution is tracked but not displayed on 1099

user_stories.title: 1099-MISC vs 1099-NEC classification
user_stories.story: Given a vendor is classified as 'rents' in the system, When 1099 generation runs, Then a 1099-MISC is generated; given a vendor classified as 'subcontractor labor', When 1099 generation runs, Then a 1099-NEC is generated
acceptance_criteria: Vendor master includes form_type field (1099-MISC or 1099-NEC)
System populates correct form type based on vendor classification
Controller can override form_type in draft mode
Box amounts are populated per IRS form specifications

user_stories.title: 1099 review and adjustment workflow
user_stories.story: Given 1099s have been generated in DRAFT status, When the controller opens the 1099 review page, Then they see vendor name, TIN, amount, and can adjust amounts with audit trail
acceptance_criteria: Review page lists all DRAFT 1099s with sortable columns
Controller can edit total_payments and box_amounts
Edits are logged with user ID and timestamp
Can save as DRAFT and return later
Can mark as REVIEWED when satisfied

user_stories.title: IRS FIRE format export
user_stories.story: Given 1099s are in REVIEWED or FILED status, When the controller clicks 'Export to FIRE', Then the system produces an IRS FIRE-format electronic filing file (.txt) ready for upload to IRS e-Services
acceptance_criteria: Export file follows IRS 1099 FIRE format specifications
Includes transmitter, vendor, and amount records
File is validated against IRS schema before download
Error messages if validation fails
Status updated to FILED with filing timestamp

user_stories.title: PDF generation and printing
user_stories.story: Given a 1099-NEC has been finalized, When the controller clicks 'Download PDF', Then a PDF is generated with official IRS 1099-NEC form layout including all copies (1, 2, B, C)
acceptance_criteria: PDF matches official IRS 1099-NEC form layout
All 4 copies are included in PDF
QR codes are not required
File is downloadable and print-ready
Font sizes and spacing match IRS specifications

user_stories.title: Amended 1099 / correction workflow
user_stories.story: Given a 1099 was filed but an error is discovered, When the controller marks it as 'CORRECTED', Then a new 1099-X (corrected form) can be generated with reference to the original
acceptance_criteria: Original 1099 is marked as VOID
1099-X is generated with corrected amounts
1099-X references original TIN and amount
Audit trail shows correction date and reason
Both VOID and 1099-X are available for filing

user_stories.title: Commission plan configuration per employee
user_stories.story: Given a sales manager wants to set commission for a new employee, When they create a commission plan with employee, plan type (flat/percentage/tiered), rate, and effective date, Then the system stores the plan and applies it to all deals posted after the effective date
acceptance_criteria: Plans can be FLAT (fixed amount), PERCENTAGE (of gross profit), or TIERED (by profit ranges)
Effective date can be past or future
Multiple plans can exist for same employee at different dates
Plan is applied based on deal posting date
Manager can update/deactivate plans

user_stories.title: Automatic commission accrual on deal posting
user_stories.story: Given a vehicle sale deal with gross profit of $5,000 is posted for employee John Smith (PERCENTAGE 2%), When the deal posts to GL, Then commission amount ($100) is automatically calculated and a GL journal entry is created
acceptance_criteria: Commission = gross_profit × commission_rate
GL entry: DR Commission Expense, CR Commission Payable
Entry flows through GL Integrity Agent validation
Commission record status = ACCRUED
Audit trail records deal_id, employee_id, commission_amount

user_stories.title: Multi-department commission support
user_stories.story: Given a used vehicle sale includes F&I products sold by F&I Manager, When the deal posts, Then sales commission accrues for the vehicle to Sales Rep and F&I commission accrues separately for F&I products to F&I Manager
acceptance_criteria: Commission plans can be department-specific (SALES, F&I, SERVICE)
Each department has its own commission payable GL account
Multi-department deals create multiple commission records (one per dept)
GL entries are isolated by department

user_stories.title: Commission payout during payroll
user_stories.story: Given bi-weekly payroll is processed for a commission-based employee, When the payroll batch is validated, Then accrued commissions for the pay period are retrieved and included in the payroll
acceptance_criteria: Payroll batch automatically includes commission line items
Taxes (FICA, MEDICARE, FUTA, SUTA) are calculated on commission
Accrued commission GL account is reversed when payout is posted
GL entry: DR Commission Payable, CR Cash
Audit trail links commission records to payroll batch

user_stories.title: Commission dashboard and reporting
user_stories.story: Given a sales manager opens the commission dashboard, When they select period and employee, Then they see YTD commissions, monthly breakdown, top deals, and commission plan details
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows YTD, monthly, and deal-level data
Filters by employee, department, vehicle type, period
Top 3 deals by commission amount displayed
Plan rate and effective date visible
Data refreshes real-time as deals are posted

user_stories.title: Commission adjustment and dispute resolution
user_stories.story: Given a sales rep disputes their commission amount, When the controller reviews and agrees and creates an adjustment with reason, Then a GL journal entry is created and the adjustment is logged with audit trail
acceptance_criteria: Controller can manually adjust commission amounts
Adjustment creates new GL entry (debit payable, credit adjustment account)
Reason is logged with timestamp and user ID
Original commission record is marked ADJUSTED
Email notification sent to employee explaining adjustment

user_stories.title: Chargeback management and commission reversal
user_stories.story: Given a vehicle sale deal (with $5,000 commission accrued) is chargebacked, When the deal status changes to CHARGEBACKED, Then the system automatically creates a reversal GL entry and commission record status is set to CHARGED_BACK
acceptance_criteria: System detects deal chargeback event
Queries and reverses commission record for that deal
GL entry: DR Commission Payable, CR Commission Expense (reversal)
Commission record status = CHARGED_BACK
If commission was already paid, creates receivable entry

user_stories.title: Monthly commission summary report
user_stories.story: Given it is month-end, When the controller runs the Commission Summary Report, Then a report is generated grouped by Employee and Department with YTD, monthly, and comparison data
acceptance_criteria: Report columns: Employee, Deal Count, Gross Profit, Commission Accrued, Paid, YTD, Plan Rate
Grouped by department with subtotals
Grand total across all employees
Prior month comparison
Exportable to CSV and email-deliverable

user_stories.title: Floor plan unit registration
user_stories.story: Given a new vehicle arrives on lot and is floored with a lender, When the office manager enters VIN, lender, advance amount, interest rate, and floor date, Then the system creates a floor plan unit record with status=ACTIVE
acceptance_criteria: Unit record includes all required fields (VIN, lender, advance, rate, date)
VIN must be unique per tenant
GL accounts are mapped for liability and interest
Status defaults to ACTIVE
Curtailment schedule can be configured

user_stories.title: Daily interest accrual batch job
user_stories.story: Given 50 vehicles are on floor, When the scheduled daily interest accrual job runs at 6 AM, Then interest is calculated for each unit and a GL journal entry posts accrual to interest expense
acceptance_criteria: Job runs daily at 6 AM (configurable)
Interest = (current_balance × annual_rate / 365)
Compound interest supported (configurable by lender)
GL entry: DR Floor Plan Interest Expense, CR Floor Plan Payable
Entry flows through GL Integrity Agent
Job logs all calculations and success/failure

user_stories.title: Interest calculation methodology
user_stories.story: Given a unit floored with advance $25,000 and rate 6.5%, When daily accrual runs, Then interest compounds correctly per lender configuration (daily or monthly)
acceptance_criteria: Daily interest = (balance × rate / 365)
System supports daily and monthly compounding
Lender config specifies compounding method
Accrued_interest field increments correctly
No rounding errors (use NUMERIC(15,2))

user_stories.title: Automatic payoff on vehicle sale
user_stories.story: Given a floored vehicle is sold, When the sale deal posts to GL, Then the floor plan liability is reversed and a payoff check is scheduled to the lender
acceptance_criteria: System matches vehicle VIN to floor plan unit
Payoff amount = original advance + accrued interest
GL entry: DR Floor Plan Payable, CR Cash
Unit status changed to PAID_OFF
AP check scheduled for lender payoff
Audit trail records payoff date and sale deal ID

user_stories.title: Curtailment tracking and payment scheduling
user_stories.story: Given a floor plan lender requires monthly curtailment, When the unit is registered with curtailment schedule, Then the system creates recurring AP reminders and automatically processes payments
acceptance_criteria: Curtailment schedule stored as {monthly: $500} or {date: $X}
Reminders created for each curtailment due date
Payment reduces floor plan liability and posts GL entry
GL entry: DR Floor Plan Payable, CR Cash
Audit trail records each curtailment payment

user_stories.title: Floor plan aging report
user_stories.story: Given it is month-end, When the controller runs the Floor Plan Aging Report, Then all ACTIVE units are shown grouped by lender with aging buckets
acceptance_criteria: Report shows: VIN, Vehicle Make/Model, Advance, Accrued Interest, Days on Lot
Grouped by lender with subtotals
Vehicles sorted by Days on Floor (oldest first)
Flags vehicles on floor >180 days (aged)
Emailed to Finance Director

user_stories.title: Multi-lender support with account isolation
user_stories.story: Given the dealership has floor plans with 3 different lenders, When units are registered, Then each lender's transactions post to their respective GL accounts
acceptance_criteria: Lender config includes: name, GL payable account, GL interest account, interest calc method, curtailment terms
Each lender has isolated GL accounts (no commingling)
Aging report and payoff amounts group by lender
Transactions are tagged with lender_id for audit

user_stories.title: Floor plan loss mitigation (damage/total loss)
user_stories.story: Given a floored vehicle is damaged or totaled, When the office manager marks the unit status as DAMAGED, Then a GL loss write-off entry is created and status changes to PAID_OFF
acceptance_criteria: Unit status can be marked DAMAGED or TOTAL_LOSS
GL entry: DR Floor Plan Loss (GL 6000), CR Floor Plan Payable
Loss amount = current balance (advance + accrued interest)
Status changed to PAID_OFF
Insurance claim workflow triggered if insured

phase_2_stories.title: FIFO inventory layer tracking
phase_2_stories.description: Track inventory purchases as layers; FIFO pops oldest layer on sales

phase_2_stories.title: Weighted-average inventory valuation
phase_2_stories.description: Calculate inventory COGS using weighted-average cost method

phase_2_stories.title: Fixed asset registration and depreciation
phase_2_stories.description: Register fixed assets with useful life and calculate monthly depreciation

phase_2_stories.title: Asset disposal and gain/loss recognition
phase_2_stories.description: Record asset disposal and calculate gain/loss on sale

phase_2_stories.title: Warranty reserve accrual on sales
phase_2_stories.description: Automatically accrue warranty liability based on historical claims ratio

phase_2_stories.title: Warranty claims against reserve
phase_2_stories.description: Record warranty claims and reduce reserve balance

phase_2_stories.title: OEM claim feed ingestion
phase_2_stories.description: Ingest daily OEM warranty claim feeds from GM, Ford, etc.

phase_2_stories.title: Auto-matching AR to OEM claims
phase_2_stories.description: Match warranty AR entries to OEM claims by claim number and amount

redesign_stories.title: GL Exception Detection Agent
redesign_stories.description: Deploy Claude agent to detect GL exceptions (anomalies, miscodings) in real-time with context-aware severity

redesign_stories.title: Deal Profitability via T1 Copilot
redesign_stories.description: Enable T1 Copilot to query GL for deal-level profitability analysis in natural language

redesign_stories.title: Bank Reconciliation AI Auto-Match
redesign_stories.description: AI agent auto-matches bank transactions to GL with confidence scoring; human reviews only low-confidence items

platform_stories.title: Multi-Currency Support (Solera Integration)
platform_stories.description: AMACC posts multi-currency transactions; Solera handles FX revaluation and consolidation

platform_stories.title: Consolidated Group Reporting (Solera Integration)
platform_stories.description: AMACC publishes per-tenant GL; Solera aggregates and consolidates across dealer group

platform_stories.title: Budgeting & Forecasting (Solera Integration)
platform_stories.description: AMACC records actuals; Solera manages master budget, variance analysis, rolling forecast

platform_stories.title: Advanced Compliance Reporting (Solera Integration)
platform_stories.description: AMACC publishes audit trail; Solera applies compliance rules and generates regulatory reports

drop_stories.title: Intercompany Billing Rules Engine
drop_stories.justification: Low adoption, complex configuration. Manual IC journal entries sufficient.

drop_stories.title: Custom Aging Report Buckets
drop_stories.justification: Standard 30/60/90 buckets address 95% of use cases. Excel export for custom analysis.
